untranslatable_reason: Signal requires direct access to Greek values (gamma, vega, theta) and IV percentiles which are not available in the allowed indicator set. The source material provides explicit delta/gamma/theta/vega calculations from options positions, but no equivalent synthetic Greeks can be reliably constructed from price-action indicators alone. Substituted hvol_20 (historical volatility) and india_vix (volatility index) as proxies for elevated volatility environment, but this does not capture the critical negative gamma/vega with positive theta structure that defines the strategy. A proper implementation requires options Greeks input.
translation_notes: This signal fundamentally requires options Greeks (gamma, vega, theta) and implied volatility metrics that fall outside the price-action indicator constraint. Entry conditions mapped to high volatility proxies (hvol_20 > 75th percentile, india_vix elevated) as substitute for 'IV percentile > top quartile', but the core short volatility spread logic cannot be properly expressed without Greeks. Exit condition uses hvol_20 mean-reversion as proxy for 'IV falls'. Recommend feeding Greeks directly into backtest engine rather than attempting synthetic approximation.
